recommendation.title: Script Enumeration (Critical CVE Follow-up)
recommendation.reason: Critical CVEs detected. NSE scripts can confirm service details and gather additional intelligence for remediation planning.
recommendation.command_description: Runs default NSE scripts for deeper service enumeration.

alternatives.title: Deep Version Detection
alternatives.reason: Outdated versions detected. A deep version scan will precisely fingerprint service versions for accurate CVE matching.

alternatives.title: UDP Top-100 Scan
alternatives.reason: UDP ports not yet scanned. Services like DNS (53), SNMP (161), and NTP (123) run over UDP and are commonly overlooked.
alternatives.command_description: Scans the top 100 UDP ports for common services.

explanation.summary: Scan complete. Found 1 live host(s) with 23 open port(s). 1 CRITICAL finding(s) — immediate action required. 2 HIGH finding(s) — remediation strongly recommended.

findings.what_was_found: Port 21/tcp is open running FTP (vsftpd 2.3.4)
findings.why_it_matters: File transfer — often transmits credentials in plaintext
findings.version_status: vsftpd 2.3.4 is end-of-life and no longer receives security patches. Replace immediately.
top_cves: CVE-2011-2523 (CVSS 10.0, CRITICAL): Backdoor in vsftpd 2.3.4. A shell is spawned when a username ending in ':)' is used. This was a supply-chain compromise of the official source.
CVE-2021-3618 (CVSS 7.4, HIGH): ALPACA cross-protocol attack. TLS traffic intended for one service may be redirected to FTP.
findings.risk_explanation: Risk classified as CRITICAL (score 8.5/10). Critical CVSS score of 10.0 — severe vulnerability present Service criticality is high Running an end-of-life version with no security patches Host has high overall exposure 2 known CVE(s) mapped
guidance: Upgrade FTP to the latest stable version immediately.
Replace FTP with SFTP. FTP credentials are sent in plaintext.
If FTP must run, restrict to a specific IP range.
Patch: Remove vsftpd 2.3.4 immediately. Deploy vsftpd 3.0.5 or later.
Patch: Enable strict TLS SNI checking. Upgrade vsftpd to 3.0.5.

findings.what_was_found: Port 22/tcp is open running SSH (OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian 8ubuntu1)
findings.why_it_matters: Remote administration — high-value target
findings.version_status: OpenSSH 4.7p1 Debian 8ubuntu1 is outdated. Upgrade recommended.
findings.risk_explanation: Risk classified as MEDIUM (score 4.0/10). Service criticality is high Version is outdated Host has high overall exposure
guidance: Upgrade SSH to the latest stable version immediately.
Disable root SSH login (set PermitRootLogin no in sshd_config).
Use SSH key-based authentication instead of passwords.
Restrict SSH access by IP using AllowUsers or firewall rules.

findings.what_was_found: Port 23/tcp is open running TELNET (Linux telnetd)
findings.why_it_matters: Plaintext remote access — must not be exposed
findings.version_status: Version not detected. Run a version detection scan for accurate CVE mapping.
findings.risk_explanation: Risk classified as MEDIUM (score 4.7/10). Service criticality is critical Host has high overall exposure
guidance: Disable Telnet immediately — it transmits all data including passwords in plaintext.
Replace with SSH.

findings.what_was_found: Port 25/tcp is open running SMTP (Postfix smtpd)
findings.why_it_matters: Mail relay — check for open relay misconfiguration
findings.version_status: Version not detected. Run a version detection scan for accurate CVE mapping.
findings.risk_explanation: Risk classified as LOW (score 3.2/10). Host has high overall exposure

findings.what_was_found: Port 53/tcp is open running DOMAIN (ISC BIND 9.4.2)
findings.why_it_matters: DNS — can be abused for amplification or poisoning
findings.version_status: ISC BIND 9.4.2 is outdated. Upgrade recommended.
findings.risk_explanation: Risk classified as MEDIUM (score 4.0/10). Service criticality is high Version is outdated Host has high overall exposure
guidance: Upgrade DOMAIN to the latest stable version immediately.
Restrict DNS zone transfers to authorised secondary servers only.
Enable DNSSEC for DNS integrity.
Rate-limit DNS queries to mitigate amplification attacks.

findings.what_was_found: Port 80/tcp is open running HTTP (Apache httpd 2.2.8)
findings.why_it_matters: Web service — attack surface depends on the application
findings.version_status: Apache httpd 2.2.8 is end-of-life and no longer receives security patches. Replace immediately.

guidance: Upgrade MYSQL to the latest stable version immediately.

Disable remote root login. Use application-specific DB accounts.
Enable MySQL audit logging.
Patch: Upgrade to MySQL 5.5.24 or later.
Port 3306 should not be publicly accessible. Restrict via firewall to authorised hosts only.
